A NWS Storm Survey concludes an EF-U tornado occurred southwest of Portage. Multiple videos and calls demonstrate a brief touchdown along Interstate 39. Surveys could not find damage along any roadways, suggesting touchdown may have happened in the Baraboo River Waterfowl Production Area or Pine Island State Wildlife area north of Hwy 33.

A NWS storm survey found EF-1 rated damage from a tornado that began west of Lowell. This tornado was on the ground for approximately 6.7 miles, ending just south of Oak Grove. Peak wind speeds were estimated to be 90 MPH.

A NWS storm survey found EF-2 rated damage from a tornado that began west of Juneau. This tornado was on the ground for approximately 9.2 miles, ending just east of Horicon. Peak wind speeds were estimated to be 120 MPH.

A NWS story survey found EF-1 rated damage from a tornado that began near the Dodge County Airport, north of Juneau. This tornado proceeded to the east before ending just east of the Rock River. Path length is estimated to be 4 miles. Peak wind speed was 95 MPH.

NWS Storm Survey: EF-1 tornado, 100mph peak winds, 50 yards wide, path 2.1 miles. Initially part of the Mayville tornado path, adjustments were made based on more information and a follow up survey which shows two distinct paths. Damage is mainly to trees northeast of the Highway 28-175 roundabout.

NWS storm survey confirms an EF-1 beginning near the intersection of Hwy YZ and Z, northeast of Dodgeville around 12:24 PM and ending near Knobs Rd around southwest of Hyde around 12:40 PM. Estimated winds of 95 mph. Tree damage and damage to outbuildings noted. Time estimated from radar.
Confirmed via County EM.
Delayed report: Tree damage near intersection of Griffiths Road and Co Hwy ZZ. Possible tornado damage pending survey.

NWS Storm Survey confirms an EF0 tornado beginning near the intersection of Racek Rd and WI-78. Max wind speed 80 MPH, with tree & shingle damage noted. Beginning time estimated from radar data. Additional info regarding path length and end time to follow in a Public Information Statement.
Crossing WI HWY 78.

NWS survey crew confirms a brief EF-0 tornado with estimated winds around 65 mph in corn fields and tree lines near Buckhorn Rd and Breezy Point Rd. This tornado started and ended around 2:59 PM CDT (radar estimated).
Video from US HWY 151 looking W.
Two large branches broken off a 10 year old tree.
NWS survey crew estimates 60 mph downburst winds based on minor tree damage in the Midnight Sun Dr neighborhood.
Public report & photographs of tree damage off WI-78 in the WI DNR Black Hawk Ridge Unit. Time estimated via radar data.
NWS survey crew estimates 70 mph downburst winds based on three pine trees snapped and surrounding minor tree damage.

NWS survey team confirms EF-0 tornado with estimated winds of 70 mph starting near the intersection of County Road E and W Hillcrest Rd southwest of Hustisford around 3:22 PM CDT, and ending near Grey Road and Wildcat Road northeast of Hustisford around 3:36 PM CDT.
Power is out in the area.
Trees down and uprooted. Garbage bins blown 75 yards. Pea size hail. Estimated winds of 60 mph.
Report of street flooding in Fond du Lac. Relayed via mPING.
Multiple reports of trees and large branches down as well as a power pole on fire.
Menomonee Falls PD reports multiple large branches and a few 7 to 8 inch diameter trees down, with at least one of those toppling some power lines.
Delayed Report. Tree damage relayed from Sheboygan County Emergency Management. One tree uprooted which was rotted out. Other tree limb damage. Time estimated from radar.
Water reported up to the bottom of car doors.
Flooding at 113th St and Watertown Plank Rd Wauwatosa, photos on X depict cars stalled / stranded / abandoned in flooded roadways.
Lots of street flooding in New Berlin area with cars stuck in the water.
Numerous local news channels with footage of flooded roadways near I-43 and Moorland Rd intersection. Reports of stranded vehicles across the area.
Waukesha Co Emergency Managers report at least 9 responses for stranded vehicles in flood waters, between 11:48 PM and 2:13 AM (current time). One water rescue underway.
I-41/45 and County Line Rd, Underpass flooded. Additional street flooding reported in Germantown WI.
KMKE Airport Ops confirms all runways except for one are flooded, along with most of the taxiways and an underpass tunnel.
Social media post depicting stranded vehicles and water rising up to a house.

Broadcast media and social media pictures and video from online media sources report creeks over topping roads with moving water in several places through the county. Some vehicles were stranded on and near bridges over these creeks. Time is a rough estimate from radar and reports.
One lane washed out/eroded south of Linnie Lac near School Drive and Gold Drive.

Update on the flooding across Waukesha County. Multiple dams across the county are overtopping or at capacity. Rivers and creeks continue to rise. Many smaller creeks are out of their banks. Multiple homes with flooded basements. Commercial and industrial facilities have also been impacted by flooding. There have been water rescues for people driving into flood waters and low crossing zones. Impacts are county wide.

Update on the flooding across Milwaukee County. Widespread road closures with flooded roadways and creeks out of their banks. Multiple home with water in basements and some properties inundated with standing water. Rivers and creeks are continuing to rise.
I-43 north (right lane) and part of County Hwy 60s closed due to water on the road.
Update on the flooding in Ozaukee County. Multiple roadways flooded. Rivers and creeks are continuing to rise. Some homes have water in their basements.

Update on the flooding in Washington county. Widespread road flooding and multiple homes with water in their basements. Some commercial and industrial areas/structures have been impacted by flooding. Rivers and creeks are continuing to rise.
Lilly Road just north of Good Hope has partially collapsed/washed out due to flooding.
Uprooted tree north of Sullivan. Other minor tree branches down in the city.
About a half a dozen large branches and tops of trees broken off along with two downed/split trees.
